search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae breathlessness shortness of breath emergency clinical guideline UAE
site:england.nhs.uk adult breathlessness pathway assessment red flags
site:who.int ICRC basic emergency care breathing assessment disposition
candidate_sources_rejected: condition-specific asthma, COPD, pneumonia, pulmonary embolism, heart failure and anaphylaxis pathways
automatic oxygen, imaging, blood-gas, medication, admission or ICU pathways
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A generic breathlessness note cannot infer one cause from symptoms.
Investigations, oxygen, medicines, escalation and disposition require explicit clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Exact chronic-source coverage is limited to adults with persistent breathlessness; WHO material adds broad acute-care documentation but not a breathlessness-specific diagnostic pathway. Paediatric, pregnancy or postpartum, postoperative, traumatic, frail and immunocompromised presentations require separate review.
setting_applicability: Emergency documentation with partial support from a chronic primary-care pathway and general first-contact emergency guidance.
UAE_applicability: The general assessment domains transfer, but UAE emergency triage, oxygen, imaging, laboratory, respiratory, cardiology, obstetric, paediatric, transfer and admission policy governs.
recency_verification: NHS England 2023 and WHO/ICRC 2018 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current registered official versions were identified; no UAE-wide exact emergency breathlessness guideline was found.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NHS England chronic adult breathlessness assessment section supports onset, progression, functional impact, associated-history, relevant-negative and clinician-risk documentation for persistent adult breathlessness; it does not cover acute, paediatric, pregnancy, postoperative or traumatic presentations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies history, physical examination, and clinician-selected initial investigations for chronic persistent breathlessness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact key-points section supports recording measured vital signs, clinician-performed chest or cardiovascular assessment and already reviewed investigation status without diagnosing a cause or ordering tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies onset and associated history, functional impact, exposures, medication context, vital signs, respiratory effort, cardiac and chest examination, and selected investigations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO/ICRC emergency history and disposition material supports clinician-recorded current severity, appearance, abnormal observations, plan, discussion and handover in acute care; it does not determine the diagnosis, intervention, urgency or disposition.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The red-flag section was reviewed as assessment-prompt evidence only; no red flag, diagnosis or escalation is selected automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists chest pain, haemoptysis, cyanosis, inability to speak in sentences, confusion, unilateral leg swelling, stridor, rapid progression, and low oxygen saturation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Acute-versus-chronic distinction, ability to speak, orthopnoea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, respiratory and thromboembolic context, pregnancy, trauma, detailed observations, blood gas, microbiology, reassessment, diagnosis, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
